categories.title: Linoprint
categories.description: Hand-cut relief prints carved from linoleum — dense ink, bold contrast, and the slow rhythm of the blade. Each edition is printed by hand.

categories.title: Paintings
categories.description: Acrylic and mixed media on canvas — figurative work exploring the body, intimacy, and colour as emotional register. Small formats and large gestures.

categories.title: Commissions
categories.description: Unique pieces made for collectors and clients — portraits, symbolic imagery, and personal narratives rendered in Larissa's visual language.

works.title: Woman & Wolf
works.description: A woman and a wolf merge in a dense, dreamlike composition. Cut by hand from linoleum, this piece explores the bond between the feminine and the wild — two forces that have never truly been separate.

works.title: Mini Canvas Series
works.description: Four small-format paintings that together form one meditation on circular form and warm colour. Geometric arcs in roasted pink and dusty lavender — quiet, repetitive, contemplative.
works.alt: Mini canvas series in roasted pink

works.title: The Eye
works.technique: Oil and mixed media on canvas
works.description: From a heavily textured white surface, a single eye looks out — framed in deep red, iris in teal. Raw, intimate, and quietly unsettling. A study in isolation and visibility.
works.alt: Mixed media — an eye on textured canvas

works.title: Rider
works.description: A woman rides through a world of flat colour and flowing form — terracotta, teal, off-white. A painting about freedom, movement, and the body in motion through an invented landscape.
works.alt: Painting — woman riding a horse in flat colour

works.title: Intimacy
works.description: Hands hold a strip of fabric against warm ochre bodies. The everyday becomes tender. A small painting about the quiet intimacy of ordinary moments and the eroticism of the mundane.

works.title: Commissioned Portrait
works.description: A woman holds a white bird against her chest, crowned by a golden halo on a dusty pink ground. Painted as a commission, this piece celebrates the sacred in the everyday feminine.
